behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the lint rule logic itself (string/AST match of template content vs forbidden phase-instruction patterns). MOCK-MISS: this behavior can fail inside one checker function without module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model judgment. Rejected higher level: behavior is explicitly a static lint check, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint rule on template fixtures containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md and on clean templates; assert reject/accept outcomes and precise violation locations.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries: template interpolation + fixture injection + assembly pipeline. Unit is too low because leakage can be introduced only after composition. REAL-INFRA: no real external infra needed; in-process harness wiring is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior is prompt assembly validation, not live model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompt assembly with production interpolation/fixtures and assert final prompt text does not contain any banned phase-instruction spans derived from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is orchestration/source-of-truth handoff (file loader -> invocation builder -> test runner). MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module wiring, not just one function. REAL-INFRA: local file loading is enough; no real network/subprocess requirement. Rejected Agentic: behavior text focuses on where context comes from (plugin/skill files vs hard-coded prompt rules), which can be proven by controlled harness wiring and fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the invocation path using real plugin/skill file loading and assert the built request context changes when skill files change while test prompt text remains constant; verify no hard-coded phase-rule injection path is used.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is a harness configurability and fixture-switching behavior across modules (variant selection + prompt immutability), not model-quality evaluation. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency beyond local fixtures. LLM-DEP: not required to verify capability to run unchanged prompt across variants. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior is about setup invariance, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness invocations (normal skill and degraded/missing SKILL.md) with identical user-style prompt text and assert only skill-context source changes via fixture/config, not prompt template content.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing with skill-context degradation; deterministic stubs would hide the causal failure (deterministic-assertion trap). MULTI-STEP: this is a single prompt-response judgment, so Workflow not required. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly requires failure driven by removed/degraded SKILL.md under unchanged prompt, which is about model decision quality under context change.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live model runs with identical user-style prompt against baseline vs degraded/missing SKILL.md context; assert baseline passes structural behavior checks and degraded variant fails at statistically reliable rate.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is pipeline ordering across modules (model client -> artifact sink -> parser). Needs wiring test, not just single function. REAL-INFRA: local artifact persistence/log sink can be exercised in-process; no real OS primitive beyond normal local file write semantics. LLM-DEP: model content quality not under test, so stubbed response is fine. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow accordingly.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a known raw response from the model client test double, run the harness, and assert diagnostic artifact contains exact raw payload before parser/extractor hooks execute (including parser-failure path).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: This is assertion-logic correctness within one validator boundary. MOCK-MISS: no cross-module interaction is required to catch keyword-only false positives. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejected Integration+: behavior targets rule semantics in the assertion engine itself.
behaviors.test_description: Test validator functions with crafted outputs that include required keywords but violate schema paths/role placement, and confirm failures; include structurally correct cases that pass without relying on keyword heuristics.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection based on actual model outputs. Mocking would often miss real output-shape drift and overfit to fixtures. MULTI-STEP: single live generation evaluated once per run family, so Agentic (not Workflow). Rejected Integration because the failure mode is tied to real model output variability/content, not just harness plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model tests on prompts known to produce occasional keyword-present but structurally wrong outputs; assert structural validator fails those cases while a keyword-only comparator would pass, demonstrating real regression catch.
